nodes.name: anomalies
nodes.description: The anomalies table stores information about financial anomalies detected in transactions, with the primary key being the id, and key attributes including date, check_type, status, and anomaly_type. This table answers business questions related to identifying and managing financial discrepancies, and users might search for terms like anomalies, irregularities, or deviations (anomalies, ketidakteraturan, deviasi).

nodes.name: id
nodes.description: The id column holds a unique integer identifier for each anomaly record and is used as a primary key (PK) in queries to filter and join with other tables.

nodes.name: date
nodes.description: The date (tanggal) column holds the date of the anomaly occurrence and is used in queries to filter and group records based on specific time periods, serving as a timestamp for tracking anomalies.

nodes.name: check_type
nodes.description: The check_type column holds the type of check performed on financial transactions and is used in queries to filter anomalies based on specific check types, serving as a status indicator (tipe pemeriksaan).

nodes.name: status
nodes.description: The status (status) column holds the current state of the anomaly and is used in queries to filter results based on the anomaly's condition, serving as a status indicator (metric) for the records in the anomalies table.

nodes.name: entity_type
nodes.description: The entity_type column holds the type of entity associated with the anomaly and is used in queries to filter results based on specific entity types, serving as a status indicator (tipe_entitas).

nodes.name: entity_id
nodes.description: The entity_id (entitas_id) column holds a unique identifier for each entity associated with an anomaly and is used in queries to filter and join with other tables.

nodes.name: anomaly_type
nodes.description: The anomaly_type column holds the classification of the detected anomaly and is used in queries to filter results based on specific types of anomalies (tipe anomali), serving as a non-key attribute for analysis.

nodes.name: severity
nodes.description: The severity (tingkat keparahan) column holds a textual representation of the anomaly's severity level and is used in queries to filter and group results based on the criticality of the anomalies.

nodes.name: total_trx
nodes.description: The total_trx column holds the total transaction amount associated with an anomaly and is used in queries to filter and aggregate data, serving as a metric (jumlah_trx).

nodes.name: total_revenue
nodes.description: The total_revenue column holds the total revenue amount associated with each anomaly and is used in queries to filter and aggregate data, serving as a metric (metrik) for financial analysis.

nodes.name: avg_revenue
nodes.description: The avg_revenue column holds the average revenue value associated with detected anomalies and is used in queries to filter and aggregate data, serving as a metric (metrik) for financial analysis.

nodes.name: zscore
nodes.description: The zscore column holds a double precision value representing the statistical measure of how many standard deviations an anomaly is from the mean, and it is used in queries to filter and aggregate data related to anomalies (anomali) in financial transactions.

nodes.name: threshold_value
nodes.description: The threshold_value column holds a double precision numeric value representing the predefined limit for detecting anomalies and is used in queries to filter and aggregate data related to financial irregularities, serving as a metric (nilai ambang).

nodes.name: actual_value
nodes.description: The actual_value column holds the numerical representation of the observed financial metric and is used in queries to filter and aggregate data related to anomalies, serving as a metric (nilai) for analysis.

nodes.name: expected_value
nodes.description: The expected_value column holds the anticipated monetary amount for a transaction and is used in queries to filter and aggregate data related to financial anomalies (nilai yang diharapkan).

nodes.name: deviation_pct
nodes.description: The deviation_pct column holds the percentage of deviation from expected values and is used in queries to filter and aggregate anomalies based on their severity, serving as a metric (persentase deviasi) for assessing financial discrepancies.

nodes.name: description
nodes.description: The description column holds a textual explanation of the anomaly and is used in queries to filter results based on specific characteristics of the anomaly (deskripsi).

nodes.name: recommended_action
nodes.description: The recommended_action column holds text values that suggest corrective measures for identified anomalies and is used in queries to filter results based on specific recommendations (tindakan yang disarankan).

nodes.name: impact_estimate
nodes.description: The impact_estimate column holds a double precision value representing the estimated financial impact of an anomaly, which can be used in queries to filter or aggregate data based on its significance, serving as a metric (estimasi dampak).

nodes.name: payment_provider
nodes.description: The payment_provider (penyedia_pembayaran) column holds the name of the payment service used for transactions and is utilized in queries to filter and group anomalies by the specific provider.

nodes.name: partner
nodes.description: The partner (mitra) column holds the name of the business partner associated with the anomaly and is used in queries to filter results based on specific partners or to join with other tables for detailed analysis.

nodes.name: product_name
nodes.description: The product_name column holds the name of the product associated with each anomaly and is used in queries to filter results based on specific products (produk) or to group anomalies by product for analysis.

nodes.name: service_code
nodes.description: The service_code (kode_layanan) column holds the unique identifier for the service associated with each anomaly and is used in queries to filter and join with other tables to analyze service-related issues.

nodes.name: first_detected
nodes.description: The first_detected column holds the date when an anomaly was first identified and is used in queries to filter or group records based on the detection date, serving as a timestamp (tanggal).

nodes.name: last_detected
nodes.description: The last_detected column holds the date when the anomaly was last identified and is used in queries to filter or group records based on the recency of detection (tanggal).

nodes.name: frequency_days
nodes.description: The frequency_days column holds an integer value representing the number of days between detected anomalies, and it is used in queries to filter and group results based on the frequency of occurrences, serving as a metric (frekuensi).

nodes.name: daily_master
nodes.description: The daily_master table stores daily financial transaction data for various partners, with the primary key being the id column and key attributes including date, total_trx, and total_revenue. This table answers business questions related to transaction performance and revenue generation, with important synonyms users might search including "daily transactions," "revenue," "partner," "pengguna unik," and "rasio keberhasilan."

nodes.name: id
nodes.description: The id column holds a unique integer value that serves as the primary key (PK) for identifying each record in the daily_master table and is often used in join operations with other tables.

nodes.name: date
nodes.description: The date (tanggal) column holds the specific date of each transaction record and is used to filter and group data in queries, serving as a key metric for analyzing daily financial activities.

nodes.name: partner
nodes.description: The partner column holds the name of the business partner involved in transactions and is used in queries to filter results based on specific partners (mitra), serving as a foreign key (FK) to link to partner details in related tables.

nodes.name: total_trx
nodes.description: The total_trx column holds the total number of transactions for a given day and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate transaction data, serving as a metric (metrik) for financial analysis.

nodes.name: success_trx
nodes.description: The success_trx column holds the total number of successful transactions (transaksi sukses) and is used in queries to filter and aggregate data for performance metrics, serving as a metric in the financial_db.

nodes.name: failed_trx
nodes.description: The failed_trx column holds the number of failed transactions (transaksi gagal) and is used in queries to filter and aggregate data related to transaction performance, serving as a metric for assessing operational efficiency.

nodes.name: total_revenue
nodes.description: The total_revenue column holds the total income generated for a given day and is used in queries to aggregate financial performance metrics, serving as a key metric (metrik) in the daily_master table.

nodes.name: success_rate_pct
nodes.description: The success_rate_pct column holds the percentage of successful transactions relative to total transactions and is used in queries to filter and aggregate data for performance analysis, serving as a metric (persentase keberhasilan) in the daily_master table.

nodes.name: unique_users
nodes.description: The unique_users column holds the count of distinct users for a given day and is used in queries to aggregate user activity metrics, serving as a key metric (metrik) for analyzing user engagement in the financial database.

nodes.name: daily_unique_users
nodes.description: The daily_unique_users table stores information about the number of unique users interacting with a system on a daily basis, with the primary key being the id and key attributes including date and unique_users. This table answers business questions related to user engagement trends and retention, with important synonyms users might search including "daily users," "unique users," "pengguna harian," and "pengguna unik."

nodes.name: id
nodes.description: The id column holds a unique integer identifier for each record in the daily_unique_users table, used as the primary key (PK) to filter and join with other tables in queries (id).

nodes.name: date
nodes.description: The date (tanggal) column holds the specific date for each record and is used to filter and group daily unique user counts, serving as a primary key (PK) for the table.

nodes.name: unique_users
nodes.description: The unique_users column holds the count of distinct users for a given day and is used in queries to aggregate user activity metrics, serving as a key metric (metrik) in the daily_unique_users table.

nodes.name: daily_unique_users
nodes.description: The daily_unique_users column holds the count of unique users for each day and is used in queries to aggregate user activity metrics (metrik) by date, serving as a key metric for analyzing user engagement.

nodes.name: financial_internal
nodes.description: The financial_internal table stores transaction records related to financial activities, with the primary key being id and key attributes including date, payment_provider, and total_net_revenue. This table answers business questions about revenue generation and transaction fees, with important synonyms users might search including "financial transactions," "pendapatan," "biaya platform," and "biaya admin."

nodes.name: id
nodes.description: The id column holds a unique integer value that serves as the primary key (PK) for identifying each record in the financial_internal table and is often used in join operations with other tables.

nodes.name: date
nodes.description: The date (tanggal) column holds the transaction date and is used to filter and group records in queries, serving as a timestamp for financial transactions.

nodes.name: payment_provider
nodes.description: The payment_provider column holds the name of the service used for processing payments and is utilized in queries to filter transactions by provider, serving as a status (status) indicator in the financial_internal table.

nodes.name: transaction_type
nodes.description: The transaction_type column holds the type of financial transaction (e.g., sale, refund) and is used in queries to filter results, making it a key metric for analyzing transaction patterns in the financial_internal table (tipe_transaksi).

nodes.name: item_type
nodes.description: The item_type column holds the type of financial item (e.g., service, product) and is used in queries to filter results, making it a key metric (tipe_item) for analyzing transaction data in the financial_internal table.

nodes.name: total_trx
nodes.description: The total_trx column holds the total number of transactions (jumlah transaksi) and is used in queries to aggregate transaction counts for reporting purposes, serving as a metric.

nodes.name: total_net_revenue
nodes.description: The total_net_revenue column holds the total revenue generated after deducting fees and is used in queries to filter and aggregate financial data, serving as a metric (metrik) for assessing overall financial performance.

nodes.name: net_gap
nodes.description: The net_gap column holds the calculated difference between total revenue and total fees for each transaction, and it is used in queries to filter and aggregate financial data, serving as a metric (selisih) for assessing profitability.

nodes.name: total_platform_fee
nodes.description: The total_platform_fee column holds the total fees charged by the platform for transactions and is used in queries to filter and aggregate financial data, serving as a metric (biaya total).

nodes.name: total_admin_fee
nodes.description: The total_admin_fee column holds the total administrative fees associated with transactions and is used in queries to filter or aggregate financial data, serving as a metric (biaya_admin).

nodes.name: daily_user_partner
nodes.description: The daily_user_partner table stores data about unique users interacting with various partners on specific dates, with the primary key being the id and key attributes including date, partner, and unique_users. This table answers business questions related to user engagement trends over time and partner performance, with important synonyms users might search including "daily users," "partner engagement," "pengguna unik," and "mitra."

nodes.name: id
nodes.description: The id column holds a unique integer value that serves as the primary key (PK) for identifying each record in the daily_user_partner table, and it is often used in join operations to link related data across tables (id).

nodes.name: date
nodes.description: The date (tanggal) column holds the specific date for each record and is used to filter and group data in queries, serving as a timestamp for daily user metrics.

nodes.name: partner
nodes.description: The partner (mitra) column holds the name of the partner organization and is used in queries to filter or group results based on specific partners, serving as a foreign key (FK) to link to related partner information in other tables.

nodes.name: unique_users
nodes.description: The unique_users column holds the count of distinct users for each partner on a given date and is used in queries to aggregate user data, serving as a metric (metrik) for analyzing user engagement.

nodes.name: hourly_pattern_daily
nodes.description: The hourly_pattern_daily table stores daily transaction patterns with its primary key as id, and key attributes including date, hour, total_trx, and success_rate_pct. This table answers business questions related to transaction trends over time, such as peak hours for transactions and success rates, with important synonyms including transaction patterns, pola transaksi, and tingkat keberhasilan.

nodes.name: id
nodes.description: The id column holds a unique integer value that serves as the primary key (PK) for identifying each record in the hourly_pattern_daily table and is used in queries to filter and join with other tables.

nodes.name: date
nodes.description: The date (tanggal) column holds the specific date for each record and is used to filter and group transactions in queries, serving as a primary key (PK) for identifying daily patterns in financial data.

nodes.name: hour
nodes.description: The hour (jam) column holds integer values representing the hour of the day and is used in queries to filter or group transaction data by specific hours, serving as a key metric for analyzing daily transaction patterns.

nodes.name: total_trx
nodes.description: The total_trx column holds the total number of transactions for a given hour and date, and it is used in queries to aggregate transaction data and filter results based on transaction counts (jumlah_trx).

nodes.name: success_rate_pct
nodes.description: The success_rate_pct column holds the percentage of successful transactions for a given hour and date, and it is used in queries to filter and aggregate data for performance analysis (persentase keberhasilan).

nodes.name: product_summary
nodes.description: The product_summary table stores information about product transactions, with the primary key being the id, and key attributes including product_id, product_name, total_revenue, and success_rate_pct. This table answers business questions related to product performance and sales metrics, with important synonyms users might search including "produk," "transaksi," "pendapatan," and "pembayaran."

nodes.name: id
nodes.description: The id column holds a unique integer identifier for each product summary record, serving as the primary key (PK) for filtering and joining with other tables in queries (identifikasi).

nodes.name: date
nodes.description: The date (tanggal) column holds the transaction date and is used in queries to filter or group records by specific dates, serving as a timestamp for the financial transactions in the product_summary table.

nodes.name: product_id
nodes.description: The product_id (id produk) column holds unique identifiers for each product and is used in queries to filter and join with other tables, serving as a primary key (PK).

nodes.name: product_name
nodes.description: The product_name column holds the name of the product and is used in queries to filter results, serving as a metric for identifying and analyzing product performance within the product_summary table (nama produk).

nodes.name: item_type
nodes.description: The item_type column holds the type of product (e.g., physical, digital) and is used in queries to filter or group results, serving as a status (status) indicator for transactions in the financial database.

nodes.name: type_of_payment
nodes.description: The type_of_payment column holds the method of payment used for transactions and is utilized in queries to filter results based on payment methods, serving as a status (status) indicator for financial transactions.

nodes.name: payment_provider
nodes.description: The payment_provider (penyedia_pembayaran) column holds the name of the payment service used for transactions and is utilized in queries to filter or group results based on the payment method, serving as a non-primary key attribute.

nodes.name: total_trx
nodes.description: The total_trx column holds the total number of transactions (transaksi) for each product and is used in queries to aggregate data, serving as a metric for analyzing product performance.

nodes.name: total_revenue
nodes.description: The total_revenue column holds the total income generated from sales (pendapatan) and is used in queries to aggregate financial performance metrics, serving as a key metric for analysis.

nodes.name: success_trx
nodes.description: The success_trx column holds the total number of successful transactions (transaksi berhasil) and is used in queries to aggregate data for performance metrics, serving as a metric in the product_summary table.

nodes.name: success_rate_pct
nodes.description: The success_rate_pct column holds the percentage of successful transactions for each product and is used in queries to filter and aggregate data, serving as a metric (persentase keberhasilan) for evaluating product performance.

nodes.name: unique_buyers
nodes.description: The unique_buyers column holds the count of distinct customers who made purchases and is used in queries to aggregate data for metrics related to customer engagement (pembeli unik).

nodes.name: repeat_buyers
nodes.description: The repeat_buyers column holds the count of customers who have made multiple purchases of a product and is used in queries to filter and aggregate data for metrics analysis (metrik) while serving as a non-primary key status indicator.

nodes.name: settlement_gap
nodes.description: The settlement_gap (selisih_settlement) column holds a double precision value representing the difference in settlement amounts and is used in queries to filter and aggregate financial data related to transactions.

nodes.name: purchase_mode
nodes.description: The purchase_mode column holds the method by which a product was purchased (e.g., online, in-store) and is used in queries to filter or group transactions, serving as a status indicator (mode pembelian).

nodes.name: channel_payment
nodes.description: The channel_payment table stores information about payment transactions made through various channels, with the primary key being id, and key attributes including date, partner, channel, purchase_mode, total_trx, total_revenue, and success_rate_pct. This table answers business questions related to payment performance and channel effectiveness, with important synonyms users might search including payment channel, transaksi pembayaran, saluran, and revenue.

nodes.name: id
nodes.description: The id column holds a unique integer value that serves as the primary key (PK) for identifying each record in the channel_payment table and is commonly used in join operations with other tables.

nodes.name: date
nodes.description: The date (tanggal) column holds the transaction date and is used in queries to filter and group records by specific time periods, serving as a timestamp for the channel_payment table.

nodes.name: partner
nodes.description: The partner (mitra) column holds the name of the payment partner and is used in queries to filter and group transaction data by specific partners, serving as a key attribute for analyzing payment performance.

nodes.name: channel
nodes.description: The column "channel" holds the type of payment channel used for transactions and is utilized in queries to filter or group results based on payment methods (saluran).

nodes.name: purchase_mode
nodes.description: The purchase_mode column holds the method of payment used for transactions and is utilized in queries to filter or group results based on payment types (metode pembelian).

nodes.name: total_trx
nodes.description: The total_trx column holds the total number of transactions (jumlah transaksi) and is used in queries to aggregate data for reporting purposes, serving as a metric in the financial_db's channel_payment table.

nodes.name: total_revenue
nodes.description: The total_revenue column holds the total revenue generated from transactions (pendapatan) and is used in queries to aggregate financial data for reporting and analysis.

nodes.name: success_rate_pct
nodes.description: The success_rate_pct column holds the percentage of successful transactions for each payment channel and is used in queries to filter and aggregate data, serving as a metric (persentase keberhasilan) for evaluating payment performance.

nodes.name: daily_product_channel
nodes.description: The daily_product_channel table stores daily transaction and revenue data for various products across different sales channels, with the primary key being the id and key attributes including date, product_name, and transaction/revenue figures for each channel. This table answers business questions related to daily sales performance and revenue generation, with important synonyms users might search including "daily sales," "produk," "transaksi," and "pendapatan."

nodes.name: id
nodes.description: The id column holds a unique integer value that serves as the primary key (PK) for identifying each record in the daily_product_channel table and is often used in join operations with other tables.

nodes.name: date
nodes.description: The date (tanggal) column holds the specific date for each record and is used in queries to filter results by date, group data by date for aggregation, and join with other tables based on date, serving as a timestamp.

nodes.name: product_name
nodes.description: The product_name column holds the name of the product and is used in queries to filter results based on specific products or to group data for aggregation, serving as a non-key attribute in the daily_product_channel table.

nodes.name: a0_trx
nodes.description: The a0_trx column holds the total number of transactions for a specific product channel on a given date and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate transaction data, serving as a metric (metrik) for financial analysis.

nodes.name: a0_revenue
nodes.description: The a0_revenue column holds the revenue generated from a specific product channel on a given day and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate financial data, serving as a metric (metrik) for performance analysis.

nodes.name: b3_trx
nodes.description: The b3_trx column holds the transaction count for the B3 product channel and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate data, serving as a metric (metrik) for financial analysis.

nodes.name: b3_revenue
nodes.description: The b3_revenue column holds the revenue generated from the B3 product channel and is used in queries to aggregate total revenue, filter results by specific revenue thresholds, and join with other financial metrics, serving as a metric (metrik).

nodes.name: f0_trx
nodes.description: The f0_trx column holds the transaction count for a specific product channel and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate data, serving as a metric (metrik) for financial analysis.

nodes.name: f0_revenue
nodes.description: The f0_revenue column holds the revenue generated from a specific product channel on a given day and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate financial data, serving as a metric (metrik) for performance analysis.

nodes.name: f4_trx
nodes.description: The f4_trx column holds the transaction count for a specific product channel on a given date and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate data, serving as a metric (metrik) for financial analysis.

nodes.name: f4_revenue
nodes.description: The f4_revenue column holds the revenue generated from a specific product channel on a given date and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate financial data, serving as a metric (pendapatan).

nodes.name: f5_trx
nodes.description: The f5_trx column holds the total transaction count for a specific product channel on a given date and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate transaction data, serving as a metric (metrik).

nodes.name: f5_revenue
nodes.description: The f5_revenue column holds the revenue generated from a specific product channel on a given day and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate financial data, serving as a metric (pendapatan) for performance analysis.

nodes.name: i1_trx
nodes.description: The i1_trx column holds the total number of transactions for a specific product channel on a given day and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate transaction data, serving as a metric (metrik).

nodes.name: i1_revenue
nodes.description: The i1_revenue column holds the revenue generated from a specific product channel on a given day and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate financial data, serving as a metric (pendapatan).

nodes.name: ig_trx
nodes.description: The ig_trx column holds the number of transactions for the Instagram channel and is used in queries to filter and aggregate transaction data, serving as a metric (metrik).

nodes.name: ig_revenue
nodes.description: The ig_revenue column holds the revenue generated from Instagram sales and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate financial data, serving as a metric (metrik) for performance analysis.

nodes.name: b0_revenue
nodes.description: The b0_revenue column holds the revenue generated from a specific product channel on a given day and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate financial data, serving as a metric (metrik) for performance analysis.

nodes.name: b0_trx
nodes.description: The b0_trx column holds the total number of transactions for a specific product channel on a given day and is used in queries to filter, group, and aggregate transaction data, serving as a metric (metrik) for financial analysis.

nodes.name: product_price_list
nodes.description: The product_price_list table stores information about products and their corresponding prices, with the primary key being id, and key attributes including product_id, product_name, and price. This table answers business questions related to product pricing and inventory management, with important synonyms users might search including "product price," "harga produk," and "product list," or "daftar produk."

nodes.name: id
nodes.description: The id column holds a unique integer value that serves as the primary key (PK) for identifying each record in the product_price_list table and is often used in join queries to link related data.

nodes.name: product_id
nodes.description: The product_id (id produk) column holds the unique identifier for each product and is used in queries to filter and join with other tables, serving as a foreign key (FK) to establish relationships within the database.

nodes.name: product_name
nodes.description: The product_name column holds the names of products and is used in queries to filter results and group data, serving as a descriptive attribute (nama produk).

nodes.name: price
nodes.description: The price (harga) column holds the monetary value of each product and is used in queries to filter and aggregate product prices for analysis, serving as a metric.
